I EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
I.1 Products covered by this document 
The product BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_BGC_003_012 (Product reference: https://doi.org/10.48670/moi-
00012), is a Baltic Sea biogeochemical reanalysis  covering the period January 1993 and up to as close 
as possible to present. The product  provides daily, monthly and yearly mean fields  for the following 
variables: 
 
• dissolved oxygen 
• ammonium 
• nitrate 
• phosphate 
• chlorophyll-a 
• Secchi depth (noon values) 
• net primary production (daily accumulated values) 
• pH 
• partial pressure of CO2 
 
The BAL MFC model system that was used to produce the reanalysis consists of the biogeochemical 
model ERGOM, which is online -coupled to the physical model NEMO. The model system produces 3D 
time series, covering the area of the Baltic Sea with a horizontal resolution of 1 nautical mile. Vertical-
ly, it provides 56 depth layers varying between 1 – 24 m. 
This reanalysis was released into the Copernicus Marine Service Product Catalogue in March 2023. 
The product in the Catalogue is extended in time on a yearly basis providing a homogenous time series 
of data. This "final dataset" will be available up to approximately present time minus 1 -2 years; de-
pending on the release of the needed forcing data. To provide a more "close to present" dataset we 
will, from Spring 2025 and onwards, additionally provide a monthly extension of the product with a 
coverage up to present time minus two months. These extensions, called an interim product, will be 
run by the same model system, but with the available forcing dataset at production time. These inter-
im products will therefore not provide an exact homogenous dataset over the full product time period. 
The interim product extensions will be replaced in the Product Catalogue by a final product extension 
when the forcing data are ready (approximately 1 -2 years later). Whether a product file is from an 
interim or final production is indicated in the file name.
